evals.name: single-pdf-ground-overlays
evals.prompt: I have this construction drawing PDF at /tmp/eval-data/JB0002131511/PRM0001388160/drawing.pdf — it's a 18 page Comcast/MasTec CD package for the JB0002131511 Peru Utilities job. Convert each site plan page into its own GroundOverlay in a single KMZ so I can review the route on Google Earth Pro. Skip cover, vicinity, legend, typical details, and traffic control sheets. Save the KMZ to /tmp/eval-out/single/output.kmz and let me know any sheets that need manual nudging in Google Earth Pro.
evals.expected_output: Single KMZ at /tmp/eval-out/single/output.kmz containing 7 GroundOverlay elements (one per SITE PLAN page 5..11), positioned roughly at lat 40.801, lon -86.030 to -86.015, with the rendered PNG of each site plan at high DPI. Sheet 1 should anchor from the embedded 40.801166,-86.030433 coord; sheets 2..7 chained from there.

evals.name: multi-prm-jb-folder
evals.prompt: My job folder is at /tmp/eval-data/JB0002131511_MULTI — it has multiple PRM subfolders, each containing a construction drawing PDF. For each PRM, render every SITE PLAN page as a Google Earth ground overlay and write one KMZ per PRM into that PRM's folder. Also give me a single combined KMZ for the whole job at /tmp/eval-out/multi/JB0002131511_MULTI_combined_overlays.kmz that contains every overlay from every PRM, organized by PRM folder. Use the drawing scale from the title block (1 inch = 40 feet) and skip the cover, legend, typical details, and traffic control pages.
evals.expected_output: Per-PRM KMZ in each PRM folder (e.g. /tmp/eval-data/JB0002131511_MULTI/PRM0001388160/PRM0001388160_overlays.kmz). Combined KMZ at /tmp/eval-out/multi/JB0002131511_MULTI_combined_overlays.kmz organized into folders by PRM, with overlay names prefixed by PRM identifier.

evals.name: cross-prm-ordinal-chain
evals.prompt: I'm validating the cross-PRM ordinal chaining behavior in scripts/build_overlays.py. First run `python evals/fixtures/build_synthetic_cross_prm.py /tmp/eval-data/cross_prm` to generate a synthetic 2-PRM JB folder under /tmp/eval-data/cross_prm/JB_TEST_XPRM (PRM1 has 1 site plan with an embedded 40.801166,-86.030433 coord; PRM2 has 2 site plans with no embedded coords; SITE PLAN ordinals are 1, 2, 3 contiguous across the two PRMs). Then run `python scripts/build_overlays.py /tmp/eval-data/cross_prm/JB_TEST_XPRM --multi-prm --combined`. Open the resulting combined KMZ at /tmp/eval-data/cross_prm/JB_TEST_XPRM/JB_TEST_XPRM_combined_overlays.kmz and verify three properties: (a) PRM2's SITE PLAN - 2 overlay's WEST edge longitude is approximately equal to PRM1's SITE PLAN - 1 overlay's EAST edge longitude within 1.0e-5 deg (because the chain places PRM2/sheet1's entry endpoint at PRM1/sheet1's exit endpoint geometry); (b) the bounding boxes of PRM1/SITE PLAN - 1 and PRM2/SITE PLAN - 3 do NOT intersect (those two are non-adjacent ordinals 1 and 3, separated by ordinal 2 in between); (c) re-running the same build_overlays.py command produces a byte-identical combined KMZ (compare SHA-256 of the two KMZs). Report pass/fail per assertion plus the actual values measured.
evals.expected_output: All three assertions pass. PRM2/SITE PLAN - 2 west edge ≈ PRM1/SITE PLAN - 1 east edge within 1e-5 deg. PRM1/SITE PLAN - 1 and PRM2/SITE PLAN - 3 bounding boxes do not intersect (separated by roughly the width of one site plan in longitude). Two consecutive runs produce byte-identical combined KMZ (matching SHA-256).

evals.name: build-hud-screen-overlays
evals.prompt: I'm validating scripts/build_hud.py. First run `python evals/fixtures/build_synthetic_combined_kmz.py /tmp/eval-data/hud/fixture_combined.kmz` to generate a synthetic combined KMZ with two GroundOverlays inside one Folder. Then run `python scripts/build_hud.py /tmp/eval-data/hud/fixture_combined.kmz --out /tmp/eval-out/hud/fixture_combined_hud.kmz`. Verify three properties: (a) the output KMZ contains exactly three new entries at files/hud/title_card.png, files/hud/legend.png, files/hud/north_arrow.png; (b) the output's doc.kml parses as XML and contains exactly three <ScreenOverlay> elements with screenXY at (0.01, 0.99), (0.01, 0.01), and (0.99, 0.01) for title / legend / north respectively; (c) every archive member other than doc.kml is byte-identical between input and output (compare SHA-256 of each non-doc.kml entry). Report pass/fail per assertion.
evals.expected_output: All three assertions pass. Output KMZ has the three HUD PNG entries. doc.kml has exactly three ScreenOverlay elements at the documented positions. All non-doc.kml archive members byte-identical between input and output.

evals.name: enhance-features-recolor
evals.prompt: I'm validating scripts/enhance_features.py. First run `python evals/fixtures/build_synthetic_feature_pdf.py /tmp/eval-data/features` which produces a synthetic PDF (with red aerial line + LASHED label, red UG line + DB UG TRENCH label, red filled vault square + VAULT label, red filled anchor triangle + ANCHOR label) and a combined KMZ at /tmp/eval-data/features/JB_FIXTURE_FEATURES_combined_overlays.kmz that embeds the PDF at pdfs/PRM0099000002.pdf and the rendered page PNG at images/PRM0099000002__sheet_05.png. Then run `python scripts/enhance_features.py /tmp/eval-data/features/JB_FIXTURE_FEATURES_combined_overlays.kmz --out /tmp/eval-out/features/enhanced.kmz`. Verify three properties: (a) the per-PRM summary printed to stdout shows nonzero classifications for PRM0099000002 with at least 1 aerial, 1 ug, 1 vault, and 1 anchor (the synthetic fixture has exactly one of each); (b) only images/PRM0099000002__sheet_05.png differs between input and output KMZ — every other archive member (doc.kml, pdfs/PRM0099000002.pdf) is byte-identical (compare SHA-256); (c) re-running enhance_features.py against the same input produces a byte-identical output KMZ (compare SHA-256). Report pass/fail per assertion plus the actual classification counts measured.
evals.expected_output: All three assertions pass. Per-PRM summary reports aerial >= 1, ug >= 1, vault >= 1, anchor >= 1 for PRM0099000002. Only the images/*.png entry differs between input and output. Two consecutive enhance_features.py runs produce byte-identical output (matching SHA-256).

evals.name: anchor-accuracy-stamp-off-route
evals.prompt: I'm validating that an embedded lat/lon stamp gets bound to the route polyline rather than its text bounding box. First run `python evals/fixtures/build_synthetic_off_route_anchor.py /tmp/eval-data/off_route` to generate a single-PRM JB folder where the stamp text is inserted at unrotated PDF (550, 1050) but the route runs through (400, 50)..(400, 1170). Then run `python scripts/build_overlays.py /tmp/eval-data/off_route/JB_TEST_OFF_ROUTE --multi-prm --combined --debug-coords 2>/tmp/off_route.log`. Verify three properties: (a) the debug-coords table shows the SITE PLAN - 1 anchor_x ~ 400 (not ~550) and anchor_y in {50, 1170} (not ~1050), confirming the anchor moved off the text bbox centroid and onto a route endpoint; (b) the SITE PLAN - 1 overlay's LatLonBox geographically contains BOTH route endpoints — i.e., the route entry's geographic position lies within [south,north]x[west,east] AND the route exit's geographic position likewise lies within those bounds; (c) the source string in the debug-coords output contains 'bound to nearest route endpoint' (the source field is truncated in the table; check /tmp/off_route.log for the full note). Report pass/fail per assertion plus the actual anchor_x, anchor_y, N/S/E/W bounds, and the first 80 chars of the source note.
evals.expected_output: All three assertions pass. anchor_x reported ~ 400.0, anchor_y reported as 50.0 or 1170.0. Both route endpoints' geographic positions fall inside the overlay's LatLonBox. Source note contains 'Embedded lat/lon stamp at PDF (' followed by the bbox-centroid coords and 'bound to nearest route endpoint'.
